<> Noel is a palindrome To replace the old URL with the new one in your PHP string you can use str_replace or preg_replace if you need pattern matching. For your specific case where the URL appears twice in the HTML tag str_replace works well because it replaces all occurrences. Here is the code logic. $oldUrl = "http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1"; $newUrl = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $text = "New post on the site $oldUrl

"; $result = str_replace($oldUrl, $newUrl, $text); This approach handles repeating strings automatically since str_replace replaces every instance found. If you have many different URLs to swap you might loop through an array of pairs. The key is to ensure the old string matches exactly what you want to replace.
